title: Yale - Alanine Aminotransferase
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cr2uk","text":"Summary: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"41rqv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ab9qc","text":"Procedure used to measure the Alanine Amino activity in blood, plasma, and serum. Alanine Amino (ALT) activity is measured by the enzymatically coupled reactions of ALT (to form pyruvate from alanine and α-ketoglutarate) and Lactate dehydrogenase (conversion of pyruvate to lactate with oxdiation of NADH to NAD). The rate of NAD formation is monitored by the change in absorbance at 340 nm. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Calibrate Cobas for Alanine Transaminase Activity analysis by running two assayed control serum.

Sample handling as performed by the Cobas Mira Plus. a) Pipette 16 µL of sample into a cuvette slot. b) Add 145 µL of Alanine Transaminase Reagent. c) Mixture is incubated at 37˚C and spun for 10 minutes. d) Absorbance is measured at 340 nm.
creator: Lili Liang
